agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Refactoring from shipment-level tracking to parcel-level tracking to support partial delivery detection. Currently, a Shipment is a single unit with one tracking number and one linear status (PENDING -> CONFIRMED -> SHIPPED -> DELIVERED). The business needs each Parcel within a Shipment to have its own independent lifecycle so that partial deliveries, individual parcel losses, and granular customer disputes can be detected and resolved.","bounded_context":"Shipment & Delivery Tracking","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"An aggregate root representing a customer's complete consignment. Contains one or more Parcels. Owns the relationship to the Order and the recipient. The Shipment's status is now DERIVED from the collective statuses of its Parcels, not independently set."},{"term":"Parcel","definition":"An individually trackable unit within a Shipment. Each Parcel has its own tracking number, weight, dimensions, status, and lifecycle. A Parcel is the atomic unit of physical delivery."},{"term":"Tracking Number","definition":"A unique identifier assigned to each Parcel (not the Shipment) that enables real-time location and status tracking through the carrier network."},{"term":"Shipment Status","definition":"A computed/derived status reflecting the aggregate state of all Parcels within a Shipment. Not stored independently — calculated from Parcel statuses."},{"term":"Partial Delivery","definition":"A state where at least one but not all Parcels in a Shipment have been delivered. This is the primary business scenario driving this refactoring."},{"term":"Parcel Status","definition":"The current lifecycle state of an individual Parcel: CREATED, PICKED_UP, IN_TRANSIT, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, DELIVERED, FAILED_ATTEMPT, LOST, RETURNED."},{"term":"Delivery Confirmation","definition":"Proof that a specific Parcel was delivered — timestamp, signature, photo, or GPS coordinates. Captured per-Parcel, not per-Shipment."},{"term":"Dispute","definition":"A customer claim that a delivered Parcel was not received, was damaged, or that the delivery confirmation is incorrect. Filed against a specific Parcel."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The logistics provider responsible for physically transporting and delivering Parcels. A Shipment may involve one or more carriers if parcels are split across services."},{"term":"Scan Event","definition":"A timestamped status update from the carrier network for a specific Parcel, recording location and status change."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Every Parcel within a Shipment MUST have its own unique tracking number.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without a unique tracking number per parcel, individual tracking is physically impossible. This is the foundational data integrity requirement for the entire refactoring.","testable_assertion":"Given a Shipment with N parcels, there must be exactly N distinct tracking numbers, and no two Parcels across any Shipment may share a tracking number."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A Shipment MUST contain at least one Parcel. A Shipment with zero Parcels is invalid.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A Shipment without parcels has no physical reality. An empty shipment would corrupt downstream processes (invoicing, carrier manifests, delivery tracking).","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create or persist a Shipment with an empty Parcels collection must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Shipment-level status is DERIVED from the collective Parcel statuses, never set independently.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If Shipment status could diverge from actual Parcel statuses, the system would report incorrect delivery information, breaking the core value proposition of partial delivery detection.","testable_assertion":"The Shipment status must always equal the result of applying the derivation function to its current Parcel statuses. There is no setter for Shipment status."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment derived status rules: ALL_DELIVERED when every Parcel is DELIVERED. PARTIALLY_DELIVERED when at least one Parcel is DELIVERED and at least one is not. IN_TRANSIT when at least one Parcel is IN_TRANSIT, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, or PICKED_UP and none are DELIVERED. FAILED when all non-delivered Parcels are LOST or RETURNED. CREATED when all Parcels are CREATED.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"These derivation rules encode the physical reality of multi-parcel delivery. If violated, the system misrepresents what actually happened to the customer's goods.","testable_assertion":"Given 5 Parcels where 3 are DELIVERED and 2 are IN_TRANSIT, Shipment status must be PARTIALLY_DELIVERED. Given 5 Parcels where 3 are DELIVERED, 1 is LOST, and 1 is RETURNED, Shipment status must be PARTIALLY_DELIVERED."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Parcel status transitions must follow valid lifecycle paths. CREATED -> PICKED_UP -> IN_TRANSIT -> OUT_FOR_DELIVERY -> DELIVERED is the happy path. FAILED_ATTEMPT can occur from OUT_FOR_DELIVERY and transitions back to IN_TRANSIT or to RETURNED. LOST can occur from IN_TRANSIT or OUT_FOR_DELIVERY. RETURNED is terminal. DELIVERED is terminal.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Invalid state transitions would mean the system accepts physically impossible events (e.g., a DELIVERED parcel going back to CREATED), corrupting tracking integrity.","testable_assertion":"Attempting to transition a DELIVERED Parcel to any other status must throw an InvalidTransitionError. Attempting to transition a CREATED Parcel directly to DELIVERED must throw an InvalidTransitionError."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A customer dispute can only be filed against a Parcel whose status is DELIVERED.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy. A future policy change might allow disputes for LOST parcels or parcels in other states, but currently disputes challenge proof of delivery.","testable_assertion":"Attempting to create a Dispute for a Parcel with status other than DELIVERED must be rejected with a validation error."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"When a Parcel is marked LOST, the system must record the last known location and timestamp, and the Shipment must be flagged for customer notification.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Notification and recording policies can change, but losing a parcel without capturing location data degrades service quality. This is a strong business policy, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"After a Parcel transitions to LOST, it must have a non-null last_known_location and last_known_timestamp, and a PARCEL_LOST notification event must be emitted."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Parcels within the same Shipment may be delivered by different carriers and at different times. The system must not assume all Parcels travel together.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"In physical logistics, parcels are frequently split across vehicles, routes, and even carriers. Assuming co-location would produce incorrect tracking data.","testable_assertion":"Each Parcel must independently store its own carrier_id. Two Parcels in the same Shipment may have different carrier_id values."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"A Shipment can only transition to a final Order state (e.g., trigger DELIVERED on the Order) when ALL Parcels are in a terminal state (DELIVERED, LOST, or RETURNED).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Closing out an order while parcels are still in transit would break financial reconciliation and prevent proper dispute resolution.","testable_assertion":"The Order state machine must not accept a transition to DELIVERED while any Parcel in the associated Shipment has a non-terminal status."},{"id":"BR-10","rule":"Each Parcel must independently track its own weight, dimensions, and contents description for customs, insurance, and carrier manifest purposes.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Carriers bill and insure per-parcel. Without per-parcel physical attributes, manifests are incorrect and insurance claims cannot be processed.","testable_assertion":"Every Parcel must have non-null weight_kg > 0 and a dimensions object at creation time."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A Shipment with 5 Parcels where 3 are DELIVERED and 2 are IN_TRANSIT","when":"The system computes Shipment status","then":"The Shipment status is PARTIALLY_DELIVERED"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Shipment with 5 Parcels where all 5 are DELIVERED","when":"The system computes Shipment status","then":"The Shipment status is ALL_DELIVERED and the Order is eligible to transition to DELIVERED"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A Shipment with 5 Parcels where 3 are DELIVERED, 1 is LOST, and 1 is RETURNED","when":"The system computes Shipment status","then":"The Shipment status is PARTIALLY_DELIVERED (not FAILED, because some were successfully delivered)"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A Parcel in status OUT_FOR_DELIVERY","when":"A carrier scan event reports a failed delivery attempt","then":"The Parcel transitions to FAILED_ATTEMPT and a retry or return decision is pending"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Parcel in status DELIVERED with delivery confirmation","when":"A customer files a dispute claiming non-receipt","then":"A Dispute is created against that specific Parcel, the Parcel remains in DELIVERED status, and the dispute resolution workflow begins"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A Parcel in status IN_TRANSIT","when":"The carrier reports the parcel as lost","then":"The Parcel transitions to LOST, last known location is recorded, a PARCEL_LOST event is emitted, and customer notification is triggered"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A Shipment with 5 Parcels where 4 are DELIVERED and 1 is LOST","when":"A customer views their shipment","then":"The customer sees per-parcel status showing 4 delivered and 1 lost, with the Shipment status as PARTIALLY_DELIVERED"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A Parcel in status DELIVERED","when":"A scan event attempts to transition it to IN_TRANSIT","then":"The transition is rejected with InvalidTransitionError — DELIVERED is terminal"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A new Shipment is being created","when":"The request contains zero Parcels","then":"The creation is rejected with a validation error"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A Shipment where all Parcels have reached terminal states","when":"The system evaluates order completion","then":"The Order state machine is notified that the Shipment is complete, enabling the Order to transition based on delivery outcomes (all delivered vs partial loss)"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"Two Parcels in the same Shipment assigned to different carriers","when":"Each carrier submits independent scan events","then":"Each Parcel's status updates independently based on its own carrier's events, without affecting the other Parcel"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A Parcel transitions to FAILED_ATTEMPT for the third time","when":"The system evaluates retry policy","then":"The Parcel is automatically transitioned to RETURNED (configurable retry threshold)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"All parcels LOST in a shipment","expected_behavior":"Shipment derived status is FAILED. Customer notification triggers for full shipment loss. Order cannot transition to DELIVERED — must follow loss/refund workflow instead."},{"scenario":"Single-parcel shipment","expected_behavior":"Behaves identically to the current single-tracking-number model. Shipment status mirrors the single Parcel's status exactly. No partial delivery state is possible."},{"scenario":"Carrier reports delivery for a Parcel already marked LOST","expected_behavior":"The LOST -> DELIVERED transition is invalid per the state machine. The scan event should be flagged for manual review as a potential carrier data error or recovery of lost parcel."},{"scenario":"Customer disputes a Parcel but it was delivered to a different Parcel's address (split delivery)","expected_behavior":"Dispute is recorded against the specific Parcel. The system must store the delivery address per-Parcel (not just per-Shipment) to support investigation."},{"scenario":"Parcel is RETURNED but customer wants it re-shipped","expected_behavior":"RETURNED is terminal. A new Parcel must be created within the same Shipment (or a new Shipment) with a new tracking number. The original Parcel's history is preserved."},{"scenario":"Concurrent scan events arrive for the same Parcel","expected_behavior":"State transitions must be applied with optimistic locking. If two events arrive (e.g., IN_TRANSIT and DELIVERED), the system processes them in timestamp order and rejects any that would create invalid transitions."},{"scenario":"Shipment has mix of express and standard parcels","expected_behavior":"Each Parcel may have its own service level. Carrier assignment and SLA tracking must be per-Parcel, not per-Shipment."},{"scenario":"Customer files dispute on a PARTIALLY_DELIVERED shipment for a Parcel that is still IN_TRANSIT","expected_behavior":"Dispute creation is rejected — disputes can only be filed against DELIVERED parcels. Customer must wait for final delivery status or file a different type of inquiry."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Per-parcel tracking enables accurate partial refunds (refund only lost/undelivered parcels instead of entire shipment), reduces over-refunding. Insurance claims become per-parcel, reducing claim amounts. Carrier billing reconciliation improves because discrepancies can be traced to individual parcels.","operational":"Operations teams gain visibility into which specific parcels are problematic, enabling targeted intervention instead of treating entire shipments as failed. Carrier performance can be measured per-parcel, improving SLA enforcement. Customer service can resolve disputes faster with parcel-level proof of delivery.","user":"Customers see granular delivery status instead of a single opaque status for their entire order. They know exactly which items arrived and which are still coming. Disputes are filed against specific parcels, reducing back-and-forth with support.","risk":"Data migration from single-tracking to multi-tracking is the primary technical risk — existing shipments need a migration strategy. Carrier integrations must be updated to map scan events to individual parcels. The derived status logic introduces complexity that must be thoroughly tested to avoid showing incorrect shipment status. Performance impact of querying N parcels per shipment instead of 1 status field.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Major positive — granular visibility into what was delivered and what was not. Faster dispute resolution. Accurate partial refunds."},{"group":"Customer Service","impact":"Major positive — can investigate and resolve issues per-parcel instead of per-shipment. Need retraining on new status model and dispute workflow."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Moderate positive — better visibility into partial failures. Must update processes to handle per-parcel carrier assignment and SLA tracking."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Moderate positive — accurate partial refunds and per-parcel insurance claims. Must update reconciliation processes."},{"group":"Carrier Partners","impact":"Low impact — carriers already track per-parcel internally. Integration layer must map their events to our new Parcel entity."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Significant effort — schema migration, state machine refactoring, API changes, carrier integration updates. Breaking change to existing Shipment APIs."}]},"confidence":{"level":80,"reasoning":"The domain model for parcel-level tracking is well-established in the logistics industry. The existing codebase has a clear Order state machine and Shipment validation that provide a solid foundation. The business requirement (partial delivery detection) directly implies the entity model and state transitions described. Confidence is not higher because some operational policies (retry thresholds, dispute resolution workflows, carrier-specific integration rules) would require further stakeholder input to fully specify.","high_confidence_areas":["Parcel as an independent entity with its own tracking number and lifecycle","Shipment status as a derived/computed value from Parcel statuses","Parcel state machine transitions (industry standard)","The invariant that DELIVERED and RETURNED are terminal states","The relationship between Shipment completion and Order state transitions"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact retry threshold before a Parcel is marked RETURNED (stated as 3 but this is a policy assumption)","Whether disputes should be allowed against LOST parcels in addition to DELIVERED parcels","Whether a Parcel can be added to an existing Shipment after creation (re-ship scenario)","Specific carrier integration mapping requirements","Whether the Shipment-level derived status formula covers all edge combinations exhaustively","Data migration strategy for existing single-status shipments"],"assumptions":["The existing Order state machine (PENDING -> CONFIRMED -> SHIPPED -> DELIVERED / CANCELLED) remains valid but its DELIVERED transition now depends on all-Parcels-terminal","Carriers provide per-parcel scan events that can be mapped to our Parcel tracking numbers","The system will store delivery confirmation (signature, photo, GPS) per-Parcel","RETURNED is terminal — re-shipping creates a new Parcel rather than reopening the old one","The business wants synchronous status derivation (compute on read) rather than async event-driven status updates"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

1	/**
2	 * Revenue sharing payout calculation.
3	 *
4	 * Formula:
5	 *   platformFeeAmount = transactionAmount * platformFeePercentage
6	 *   partnerPayout     = (transactionAmount - platformFeeAmount) * partnerSharePercentage
7	 *
8	 * Result is floored to the nearest cent (never overpays partners).
9	 *
10	 * @param transactionAmount      Gross transaction value in dollars (float, e.g. 100.00)
11	 * @param platformFeePercentage  Platform fee as a decimal fraction (e.g. 0.05 = 5%)
12	 * @param partnerSharePercentage Partner revenue share as a decimal fraction (e.g. 0.70 = 70%)
13	 * @returns Partner payout in dollars, floored to the nearest cent
14	 */
15	export function calculatePartnerPayout(
16	  transactionAmount: number,
17	  platformFeePercentage: number,
18	  partnerSharePercentage: number
19	): number {
20	  const platformFeeAmount = transactionAmount * platformFeePercentage;
21	  const netAmount = transactionAmount - platformFeeAmount;
22	  const rawPayout = netAmount * partnerSharePercentage;
23	
24	  // Floor to nearest cent to avoid overpaying partners (AC4).
25	  // Multiply by 100, floor, divide by 100 to preserve two decimal places.
26	  return Math.floor(rawPayout * 100) / 100;
27	}

content.text: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Refactoring from shipment-level tracking to parcel-level tracking to support partial delivery detection. Currently, a Shipment is a single unit with one tracking number and one linear status (PENDING -> CONFIRMED -> SHIPPED -> DELIVERED). The business needs each Parcel within a Shipment to have its own independent lifecycle so that partial deliveries, individual parcel losses, and granular customer disputes can be detected and resolved.","bounded_context":"Shipment & Delivery Tracking","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"An aggregate root representing a customer's complete consignment. Contains one or more Parcels. Owns the relationship to the Order and the recipient. The Shipment's status is now DERIVED from the collective statuses of its Parcels, not independently set."},{"term":"Parcel","definition":"An individually trackable unit within a Shipment. Each Parcel has its own tracking number, weight, dimensions, status, and lifecycle. A Parcel is the atomic unit of physical delivery."},{"term":"Tracking Number","definition":"A unique identifier assigned to each Parcel (not the Shipment) that enables real-time location and status tracking through the carrier network."},{"term":"Shipment Status","definition":"A computed/derived status reflecting the aggregate state of all Parcels within a Shipment. Not stored independently — calculated from Parcel statuses."},{"term":"Partial Delivery","definition":"A state where at least one but not all Parcels in a Shipment have been delivered. This is the primary business scenario driving this refactoring."},{"term":"Parcel Status","definition":"The current lifecycle state of an individual Parcel: CREATED, PICKED_UP, IN_TRANSIT, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, DELIVERED, FAILED_ATTEMPT, LOST, RETURNED."},{"term":"Delivery Confirmation","definition":"Proof that a specific Parcel was delivered — timestamp, signature, photo, or GPS coordinates. Captured per-Parcel, not per-Shipment."},{"term":"Dispute","definition":"A customer claim that a delivered Parcel was not received, was damaged, or that the delivery confirmation is incorrect. Filed against a specific Parcel."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The logistics provider responsible for physically transporting and delivering Parcels. A Shipment may involve one or more carriers if parcels are split across services."},{"term":"Scan Event","definition":"A timestamped status update from the carrier network for a specific Parcel, recording location and status change."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Every Parcel within a Shipment MUST have its own unique tracking number.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without a unique tracking number per parcel, individual tracking is physically impossible. This is the foundational data integrity requirement for the entire refactoring.","testable_assertion":"Given a Shipment with N parcels, there must be exactly N distinct tracking numbers, and no two Parcels across any Shipment may share a tracking number."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A Shipment MUST contain at least one Parcel. A Shipment with zero Parcels is invalid.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A Shipment without parcels has no physical reality. An empty shipment would corrupt downstream processes (invoicing, carrier manifests, delivery tracking).","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create or persist a Shipment with an empty Parcels collection must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Shipment-level status is DERIVED from the collective Parcel statuses, never set independently.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If Shipment status could diverge from actual Parcel statuses, the system would report incorrect delivery information, breaking the core value proposition of partial delivery detection.","testable_assertion":"The Shipment status must always equal the result of applying the derivation function to its current Parcel statuses. There is no setter for Shipment status."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment derived status rules: ALL_DELIVERED when every Parcel is DELIVERED. PARTIALLY_DELIVERED when at least one Parcel is DELIVERED and at least one is not. IN_TRANSIT when at least one Parcel is IN_TRANSIT, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, or PICKED_UP and none are DELIVERED. FAILED when all non-delivered Parcels are LOST or RETURNED. CREATED when all Parcels are CREATED.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"These derivation rules encode the physical reality of multi-parcel delivery. If violated, the system misrepresents what actually happened to the customer's goods.","testable_assertion":"Given 5 Parcels where 3 are DELIVERED and 2 are IN_TRANSIT, Shipment status must be PARTIALLY_DELIVERED. Given 5 Parcels where 3 are DELIVERED, 1 is LOST, and 1 is RETURNED, Shipment status must be PARTIALLY_DELIVERED."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Parcel status transitions must follow valid lifecycle paths. CREATED -> PICKED_UP -> IN_TRANSIT -> OUT_FOR_DELIVERY -> DELIVERED is the happy path. FAILED_ATTEMPT can occur from OUT_FOR_DELIVERY and transitions back to IN_TRANSIT or to RETURNED. LOST can occur from IN_TRANSIT or OUT_FOR_DELIVERY. RETURNED is terminal.
